title: carex vesicaria subsp. vesicata
intro: Carex vesicaria subsp. vesicata, also known as bladder sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex vesicaria plant, which is a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract problems. The health benefits of bladder sedge include its ability to relieve symptoms of kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and edema, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Bladder sedge can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support urinary health and overall well-being.
